behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the file path (packages/vite-plugin-cloudflare/src/workers/runner-worker/module-runner.ts), the function names (runInRunnerObject, executeCallback), the class name (__VITE_RUNNER_OBJECT__), and the module-scope variables (pendingCallbacks, callbackResults, nextCallbackId) — all of which are referenced in the tests. The tests check for these exact identifiers, and they are all discoverable from instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes the bug (entries not cleaned up on error) and the expected behavior (cleanup in runInRunnerObject regardless of success/failure), but does not specify the exact fix (e.g., 'add try/finally'). A developer must still reason about the implementation approach.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_error_handling_around_rpc_call requires 'try' in the function body and either 'finally' or 'catch'. An alternative correct fix using .then()/.catch() promise chaining without the keyword 'try' would fail this test. test_caller_cleans_up_pending_callbacks asserts 'pendingCallbacks.delete' must appear literally in runInRunnerObject — an alternative that uses pendingCallbacks.clear() or reconstructs the Map would fail. These tests over-constrain to the gold solution's specific implementation p

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_cleanup_on_error extracts the function body, builds a runnable JS file with mocked environments, executes it via Node.js subprocess, and checks that Maps are empty after both error and success paths. This is genuine behavioral testing. While test_caller_cleans_up_pending_callbacks and test_error_handling_around_rpc_call are text-matching, test_cleanup_on_error actually executes the code, so not every test is purely textual.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation (e.g., empty function) would fail test_not_stub (requires pendingCallbacks.set, callbackResults, nextCallbackId, executeCallback in the body) and test_cleanup_on_error (which checks that the function returns 42 and 'hello' on success path). The f2p tests require actual working logic plus cleanup behavior.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires cleanup of both pendingCallbacks and callbackResults on success and error, within runInRunnerObject itself. Tests verify: cleanup on error (test_cleanup_on_error error path), cleanup on success (test_cleanup_on_error success path), cleanup in runInRunnerObject specifically (test_caller_cleans_up_pending_callbacks), and error handling (test_error_handling_around_rpc_call). All stated requirements are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, anti-stub), descriptive function names and docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task involves fixing a memory leak in TypeScript code, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: All filenames, paths, variable names, and function names in the instruction and tests are consistent and correctly spelled.
